title: Ensure that the kubelet --config configuration file has permissions set to 600 or more restrictive

input_row.title: Ensure that the kubelet --config configuration file has permissions set to 600 or more restrictive
input_row.description: Ensure that if the kubelet refers to a configuration file with the --config argument, that file has permissions of 600 or more restrictive.
input_row.rationale: The kubelet reads various parameters, including security settings, from a config file specified by the --config argument. If this file is specified you should restrict its file permissions to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be writable by only the administrators on the system.
input_row.audit: In OpenShift 4, the kubelet configuration file is managed by the Machine Config Operator Run the following command to check the permission: for node in $(oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}') do oc debug node/${node} -- chroot /host stat -c %a /var/data/kubelet/config.json done OR for node in $(oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}') do oc debug node/${node} -- chroot /host stat -c %a /host/var/data/kubelet/config.json done Verify that the permissions are 600 .
input_row.default_value: By default, the /var/lib/kubelet/config.json file has permissions of 600 . Internal Only - General

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_ocp_kubelet_config_file_permissions_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. Use IBM Cloud CLI to enumerate all OpenShift nodes.
   - Command: `oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'`
2. For each node indicated, leverage the existing audit command provided in the control to check the permissions of the kubelet configuration file:
   - Command: 
     - `oc debug node/${node} -- chroot /host stat -c %a /var/data/kubelet/config.json` 
     - `oc debug node/${node} -- chroot /host stat -c %a /host/var/data/kubelet/config.json`
3. Validate that the permissions returned for the configuration file are `600` or more restrictive.
4. Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
   - Pass: The permissions are 600 or more restrictive.
   - Fail: The permissions are less than 600.

By implementing this approach, one can automate the process of verifying compliance with the CIS control concerning the kubelet configuration file permissions within an OpenShift environment on IBM Cloud.
